title: Cacalia ilicifolia
intro: Cacalia ilicifolia, also known as Oakleaf Cacalia, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, Cacalia ilicifolia has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Cacalia ilicifolia is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to make decoctions and tinctures.
taxonomy: Cacalia ilicifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to North America and is also known as Holly-leaved Cacalia. The plant is a perennial herb that grows in woodland environments. Its classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Cacalia, Species: C. ilicifolia. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.

Its leaves, roots, and stems have been employed in various remedies, often for their perceived antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties.
